scope: Manual assessment of the twenty most-cited OpenAlex article records matching KNIME in title or abstract.

method.input_files: Downloaded PDF files supplied under data/original/articles.
method.text_extraction: PDFs were converted with pdftotext -layout for manual reading and keyword search.
assessment_focus: how KNIME is used in each article
whether the article publishes or links a workflow artifact
whether the article reports a KNIME version
whether data, code, or extension artifacts are made available
relevance to long-term reproducibility of KNIME-based studies

limitations: The assessment is based on the downloaded PDFs and their extracted text.
External URLs, supplementary archives, and workflow files were not downloaded or executed in this pass.
Workflow availability is therefore recorded conservatively when the paper mentions a workflow but the PDF text does not prove that a downloadable KNIME workflow file is present.
No KNIME workflow import or execution tests were performed.
The article_audit_fields block was added from existing assessment notes and the OpenAlex top-cited CSV; article full texts were not reread for this restructuring pass.
Linked workflow artifact retrievability is not newly revalidated here; values distinguish existing project-note evidence from URLs that were not checked in this pass.

The article_audit_fields block is split into description_audit_fields for traceability and flag_audit_fields for statistics; flag_audit_fields now contains only presence flags, so true means the item/category is present and false means absent or unsupported by the current evidence.

flag_audit_support contains citation objects only for true presence flags; false flags intentionally have no support entry. Existing evidence line references are reused where available, while resource URLs and manual classifications use source labels. Article full texts were not reread in this pass.

articles.title: Content-aware image restoration: pushing the limits of fluorescence microscopy
articles.knime_usage_summary: The study develops content-aware image restoration methods. The experiments are reported as Python/Keras/TensorFlow work, while KNIME is presented as one user-facing way to access the developed restoration tools.

workflow_artifact.summary: The supplementary PDF includes an image of the KNIME workflow, but no downloadable KNIME workflow file was found in the assessed materials.

articles.data_availability: Training and test data are reported as available through the publication site.
articles.code_or_software_availability: Source code is reported as available through the CSBDeep GitHub repository and documentation site.

linked_resources.notes: The supplementary PDF shows a KNIME workflow image, but no separate KNIME workflow download URL was found.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: This is an influential case where KNIME appears as a distribution interface for a computational method. Because only a workflow image was found, exact KNIME reproduction would require reconstructing the workflow and resolving its software/plugin dependencies.

evidence.note: The abstract says the methods are available as open-source software in Python, FIJI, and KNIME.
The methods section reports that restoration experiments were performed in Python with Keras and TensorFlow.
The data availability section lists data, code, FIJI plugins, and KNIME workflows.
Manual assessment found a KNIME workflow image, not a downloadable workflow artifact.
The software summary reports Python, TensorFlow, Keras, Fiji/ImageJ, and public code locations.

articles.title: KNIME - the Konstanz information miner
articles.knime_usage_summary: The article describes KNIME itself, especially KNIME 2.0, including its workflow model, nodes, data tables, settings, metanodes, database access, loops, and integrations.

workflow_artifact.summary: The paper contains example workflow figures but is a platform description rather than a reproducibility case study with a downloadable workflow artifact.

articles.data_availability: Not applicable; the article is a platform paper.
articles.code_or_software_availability: The paper points readers to the KNIME website for downloads and extensions.

linked_resources.notes: The article reports KNIME platform availability, not a study dataset or workflow artifact.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: This article is useful background evidence for KNIME's workflow abstraction and extension architecture, but it does not provide a scientific workflow artifact to reproduce.

evidence.note: The abstract says the paper introduces KNIME internals and features available in version 2.0.
The overview explains workflows as nodes connected by edges, with data and node settings stored with the workflow.
The text reports support for Weka 3.5.6 and about 100 Weka nodes.
The text describes new features in KNIME 2.0.

description_audit_fields.title: KNIME - the Konstanz information miner
description_audit_fields.evidence_notes: This article is useful background evidence for KNIME's workflow abstraction and extension architecture, but it does not provide a scientific workflow artifact to reproduce.

articles.title: Scientific workflow systems: Pipeline Pilot and KNIME
articles.knime_usage_summary: The article compares KNIME and Pipeline Pilot as scientific workflow systems, especially for cheminformatics. It discusses KNIME's table-oriented workflow execution, extensions, restart behavior, and workflow exchange.

workflow_artifact.summary: The article is a review/comparison and does not report a new KNIME workflow artifact.

articles.data_availability: Not applicable; the article is a review.
articles.code_or_software_availability: Not applicable; no new software artifact is presented.

linked_resources.notes: The myExperiment URL is cited as a workflow repository in general, not as a workflow artifact produced by this review.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: The article supports the paper's framing that KNIME is used as a scientific workflow system and that workflow sharing matters, but it is not itself an executable workflow case.

evidence.note: The abstract frames the article as a comparison of Pipeline Pilot and KNIME.
The introduction says myExperiment includes KNIME workflows.
The KNIME section discusses registered users, downloads, KNIME Server, and workflow exchange.

description_audit_fields.title: Scientific workflow systems: Pipeline Pilot and KNIME
description_audit_fields.evidence_notes: The article supports the paper's framing that KNIME is used as a scientific workflow system and that workflow sharing matters, but it is not itself an executable workflow case.

articles.title: Machine learning approach using MLP and SVM algorithms for the fault prediction of a centrifugal pump in the oil and gas industry
articles.knime_usage_summary: The study builds a KNIME workflow for pump fault diagnosis using preprocessing, filtering, normalization, neural network and SVM-related modeling, and scoring nodes.

workflow_artifact.summary: The article shows and describes a KNIME workflow, but the PDF text does not provide a downloadable workflow artifact.

articles.data_availability: The paper acknowledges SARLUX for supplying the pump data; no public data repository was found in the extracted PDF text.
articles.code_or_software_availability: No public code or KNIME workflow artifact was found in the extracted PDF text.

linked_resources.notes: No data URL, data DOI, code URL, or workflow URL was found in the extracted PDF text.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: This is a direct KNIME workflow use case. Without a downloadable workflow, KNIME version, and public data, reproduction would require reconstructing the workflow from the paper and figure descriptions.

evidence.note: The abstract states that SVM and MLP were implemented in the KNIME platform.
The methods state that KNIME was adopted to set up the model workflow.
Figure 2 is described as the KNIME workflow built for fault diagnosis.
The text names several KNIME preprocessing, modeling, and scoring nodes.

articles.title: Open-source QSAR models for pKa prediction using multiple machine learning approaches
articles.knime_usage_summary: The study uses a KNIME workflow to curate and standardize chemical structures before QSAR modeling. The main modeling workflow uses additional software, including OPERA, Python, R, PaDEL, and machine learning libraries.

workflow_artifact.summary: The paper mentions a KNIME workflow for standardization, but the identified GitHub repository is for OPERA code/data and does not mention KNIME.

articles.data_availability: The paper reports that all datasets are available as additional files and on GitHub.
articles.code_or_software_availability: The OPERA GitHub repository provides code/data for the published models, but was not found to provide a KNIME workflow.

linked_resources.notes: Manual check found no KNIME mention in the OPERA GitHub repository; it is treated as code/data evidence, not workflow evidence.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: This is relevant because KNIME is part of the data-preparation chain, but the public code repository does not appear to preserve the KNIME workflow used for curation.

evidence.note: The abstract says structures were curated and standardized using KNIME.

The availability statement says datasets and code are available as additional files and on GitHub.

articles.title: The impact of engineering students' performance in the first three years on their graduation result using educational data mining
articles.knime_usage_summary: The study develops a KNIME-based educational data mining workflow with Excel import, descriptive statistics, partitioning, visualization, normalization, PCA, learner and predictor metanodes, scoring, and Excel export.

workflow_artifact.summary: The article describes the KNIME workflow in detail, but no downloadable KNIME workflow artifact was found in the extracted PDF text.

linked_resources.notes: The PDF says the spreadsheet is named Supplementary material and is available from Popoola et al. (2018) via appendix A, but no direct dataset URL is visible in the extracted text.

articles.title: KNIME-CDK: Workflow-driven cheminformatics
articles.knime_usage_summary: The article presents KNIME-CDK, an open-source KNIME plugin that exposes Chemistry Development Kit functionality as KNIME nodes for workflow-driven cheminformatics.

workflow_artifact.summary: The paper reports example workflows in myExperiment and cites a specific myExperiment KNIME workflow URL.

articles.data_availability: The paper demonstrates the plugin with the ChEBI database; no separate experimental dataset is central to the paper.
articles.code_or_software_availability: The plugin is reported as available through the KNIME update mechanism, with source releases and nightly builds under LGPLv3.

linked_resources.notes: The workflow URL is cited in the references as a MyExperiment KNIME workflow.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: This is a strong artifact case for the study because it gives KNIME version requirements, extension availability, source availability, and example workflow links. It is also exposed to long-term risks from extension compatibility and external workflow-hosting availability.

evidence.note: The abstract introduces KNIME-CDK as an open-source KNIME plugin.
The implementation section reports example workflows in myExperiment and testing on KNIME 2.6 and 2.7.
The availability section gives the project URL, update mechanism, KNIME Desktop v2.6+ requirement, and license.
The references include a myExperiment KNIME workflow URL.

description_audit_fields.title: KNIME-CDK: Workflow-driven cheminformatics
description_audit_fields.evidence_notes: This is a strong artifact case for the study because it gives KNIME version requirements, extension availability, source availability, and example workflow links. It is also exposed to long-term risks from extension compatibility and external workflow-hosting availability.

articles.title: Latest tools for data mining and machine learning
articles.knime_usage_summary: The article reviews data mining and machine learning tools, including KNIME, Weka, RapidMiner, and Orange. It does not use KNIME for an empirical workflow.

workflow_artifact.summary: No KNIME workflow artifact is expected because the paper is a general tool review.

articles.data_availability: Not applicable; no new dataset is central to the paper.
articles.code_or_software_availability: Not applicable; no new software artifact is presented.

linked_resources.notes: The visible URLs are general tool-reference links, not data or KNIME workflow artifacts.

articles.reproducibility_relevance: This record shows that OpenAlex KNIME matches include tool overviews, not only empirical KNIME-based studies. It should not be counted as evidence of a reproducible KNIME workflow.

evidence.note: The abstract frames the paper as a review of data mining and machine learning tools.
The introduction lists KNIME among the reviewed tools.
The comparison table reports KNIME metadata, including version 2.9.

description_audit_fields.description: Map of human-readable and traceability fields. These preserve identifiers, labels, simplified article-relation labels and other categorical values, version strings, and evidence notes.

flag_audit_fields.description: Map of statistics-ready presence flags for deeper workflow-reporting audit cases. A true value means the article assessment found the named item/category; a false value means that item/category was absent, not reported, not applicable, not assessed, or not verified in the current evidence. Records with knime_article_relation=about_knime keep this map empty because they are background/platform papers.

item_shape.field_name: true means presence; false means absence or no supporting evidence

flag_audit_fields.absence_and_uncertainty_storage: Absence, not-applicable, not-assessed, unclear, and not-checked classifications are preserved as strings in description_audit_fields rather than as true flags.
flag_audit_fields.knime_relation_note: Detailed KNIME presentation categories were replaced by description_audit_fields.knime_article_relation and the presence flag uses_knime.
flag_audit_fields.workflow_text_description_note: describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text is true when nodes_or_components_reported contains named KNIME workflow steps, nodes, modules, or components recorded during assessment.

flag_audit_support.description: Map from true flag_audit_fields names to citation objects supporting the presence claim. Existing evidence line references are reused where available; resource URLs and manual classifications are marked with source labels such as linked_resources, article_audit_fields, or manual_assessment.

flag_name.extracted_text_lines: extracted-text line range or source label

article_text_source.method: Generated from local PDFs with scripts/extract_article_texts.py using pdftotext -layout.
